protocol.name: lunaris_window_attach
protocol.description:
Ground-work protocol for eliminating the one-frame visible lag between a CosmicSurface (xdg_toplevel) and its shell-rendered Lunaris header.
Conceptually the shell declares "my wl_surface X should render as if it were a synchronized subsurface of the toplevel whose opaque `window_id` was last reported via the `lunaris-shell-overlay::window_header_show` event". During drag/resize the compositor then has enough local state to place the header atomically together with the window on every frame, without requiring another IPC round-trip per pointer motion.
v1 of this protocol only models the *binding* — the actual renderer integration that lets the compositor position the bound surface is a follow-up. v1 still gives us three concrete wins today:
* The compositor can prove at server boot-up that a shell is attach-aware (vs. the legacy fullscreen-layer approach). * The shell can keep attachments across theme reloads / HMR without rebuilding surfaces. * Destroy / unbound lifecycle is well-defined and becomes the forward-compat contract for phase 2 rendering.

interface.name: lunaris_window_attach_manager_v1
interface.description: Singleton global. The shell binds it once and uses it to create one `lunaris_window_attachment_v1` object per header `wl_surface` it wants the compositor to co-manage.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroying the manager does not implicitly destroy any attachments previously created through it — those remain live until explicitly destroyed by the shell.

request.name: get_attachment

description.summary: claim an attachment object for a wl_surface

request.description:
The resulting attachment is dormant until `attach_to_window` is called. The underlying `wl_surface` keeps its existing role (layer surface, etc.) and renders normally.
It is a protocol error for the shell to request two attachment objects for the same wl_surface.

interface.name: lunaris_window_attachment_v1
interface.description: Binds a shell surface to a compositor-managed window via the opaque `window_id` that the `lunaris-shell-overlay` protocol carries. Once bound, the compositor treats the attachment as declared state: on every frame the attachment can be placed at `(window.x + offset_x, window.y + offset_y)` with the declared size, independently of when the shell last committed. The renderer-side implementation of this placement is staged: v1 accepts the binding and exposes debug info only.

request.name: destroy
request.description: The underlying `wl_surface` returns to standalone rendering on the next commit. If the surface is destroyed first the attachment becomes inert and must still be destroyed explicitly.

request.name: attach_to_window

description.summary: bind this attachment to a compositor window

request.description:
`window_id` is the same opaque u32 that the `lunaris-shell-overlay::window_header_show` event carries (see `Shell::window_header_surface_id` on the compositor). `offset_x`, `offset_y` are the attachment's origin relative to the window's origin; `width` and `height` declare the attachment's logical size for the compositor's layout tracking. Calling `attach_to_window` again on a live attachment is an implicit rebind.
If the target window does not exist the attachment emits `unbound` on the next compositor tick.

arg.name: offset_x
arg.summary: attachment x offset from window origin

arg.name: offset_y
arg.summary: attachment y offset from window origin

request.name: update_geometry

description.summary: update offset and size without rebinding

request.description: Cheaper than `attach_to_window` when only the offset or dimensions change. Valid only on a currently bound attachment; otherwise silently ignored.

event.name: unbound

description.summary: the attachment target window is gone

event.description: Sent once when the window the attachment was bound to disappears (close, unmap, session-lock). The attachment object itself is still live — the shell should destroy it explicitly.
